description_long: This is a bulbous, ancient-style ceramic vase with a wide, flared mouth and a single rounded handle attached to its upper body. The surface is decorated with a series of geometric patterns, primarily diamond grids framed by red linear borders, giving it a distinctive, hand-painted appearance. The body shows signs of age, with visible discoloration, surface wear, and some darkened patches, suggesting long-term use or exposure. The handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the form, with the mouth slightly uneven, adding to its handcrafted character. The overall color palette is earthy, dominated by beige and reddish-brown tones, with the painted patterns standing out against the lighter background. This vase would be a striking decorative piece in a historical or rustic urban setting, such as a public plaza, garden, or museum display.
description: A bulbous, hand-painted ceramic vase with a flared mouth, single handle, and geometric red and beige patterns. The surface shows signs of age and wear, enhancing its antique character.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared mouth, bulbous body, and geometric red-bordered diamond patterns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single rounded handle and the continuation of the geometric pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the handle attachment and the back of the vase, with visible wear and pattern details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle and the full extent of the painted motifs.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
